market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+37.6% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+31.4% YTD) | 5 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: energy (+32.5% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+33.8% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, communication-services, information-technology | Régions en difficulté: germany, switzerland, italy | Concentration: peu de secteurs en zone favorable (rotation possible) | 5 secteurs avec 6M négatif (momentum moyen-terme dégradé)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: materials(neutral,b=0.85,d=0.03%), energy(favored,b=0.74,d=1.76%), financials(avoided,b=0.94,d=-1.63%), communication-services(avoided,b=1.12,d=-2.51%), information-technology(avoided,b=1.33,d=-1.22%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.25,d=1.34%), industrials(neutral,b=0.9,d=-0.61%), utilities(neutral,b=0.37,d=-0.81%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.52,d=-0.22%), healthcare(avoided,b=0.55,d=-1.19%), consumer-discretionary(avoided,b=1.1,d=-2.14%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $98 with 5-day avg >$104 signals persistent energy-driven inflation pressure, PCE at 2.83% well above target, while VIX rising to 22, tech/financials/consumer-discretionary all deeply negative, and only energy showing strength — classic stagflationary rotation. Fed on hold (0 delta 6m) despite above-target inflation suggests they're trapped between growth fears and sticky prices.

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap — stagflation winner, +37.6% YTD momentum confirmed by RADAR
adjustments.rationale: Brent ~$98-104, energy is the only favored sector with massive YTD outperformance; direct stagflation hedge.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/tech exposure — high beta, negative momentum, rate-sensitive
adjustments.rationale: IT sector avoided by RADAR (-1.22% daily), beta 1.33 in a rising-rate stagflationary environment is toxic for duration-heavy growth.

adjustments.action: Reduce semi cap — cyclical demand risk + tech rotation
adjustments.rationale: Semiconductors are high-beta cyclicals facing demand destruction in stagflation; RADAR confirms IT sector avoided.

adjustments.action: Increase gold hedge — despite 11% drawdown from ATH, stagflation is gold's best regime
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4728 with 11% drawdown is a buying opportunity in stagflation; real rates compressed by above-target inflation, dollar weakening (DTWEXBGS 120.6 = neutral-to-weak).

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value tilt — defensive quality outperforms in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Consumer staples (+1.34% daily) and low-beta defensives are the only non-energy sectors holding up; value/dividend provides inflation-linked cash flows.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS + shorten duration — inflation above target, Fed trapped
adjustments.rationale: PCE 2.83%, 5Y breakeven 2.56% still underpricing oil-driven inflation; TIPS protect real returns while short duration limits rate risk with 10Y at 4.33%.

adjustments.action: Raise bond floor for Stable — increase defensive allocation
adjustments.rationale: Stable at 37.9% bonds with 62.1% equity is overweight risk for a stagflationary regime; increase bond floor to reduce drawdown risk.

adjustments.action: Add healthcare as defensive — low beta, non-cyclical
adjustments.rationale: Healthcare (beta 0.55) provides defensive ballast despite RADAR avoiding it on momentum — fundamentally resilient in stagflation with pricing power.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Short-duration TIPS-heavy strategy: inflation above target with Fed paralyzed, avoid credit risk as HY spreads widen, treasury short-end for liquidity.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX at 22 and rising with regime uncertainty warrants tactical cash to buy dips if energy shock escalates or if equity capitulation creates opportunities; not excessive given T-bill yields ~4% in existing bond allocations.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that correlates with tech/growth in stress; stagflation + rising VIX = reduce to minimal speculative allocation only in Agressif.

warnings: Oil supply shock escalation: Brent 5-day avg $104 vs spot $98 suggests recent spike — if geopolitical trigger (Middle East, Russia) pushes Brent >$110, regime shifts toward crisis
HY spread widening trend (312bps, widening) — if HY crosses 400bps, credit stress becomes systemic and regime shifts to risk_off/crisis
Fed credibility trap: PCE 2.83% with 0 rate changes in 6 months — market may force repricing of hikes, spiking front-end yields and crushing equity multiples
S&P +3.9% daily bounce looks like a bear market rally / short squeeze — do NOT chase; today's bounce amid negative sector breadth (6/11 sectors red) is not sustainable
EUR/USD at 1.167 signals dollar weakness — if DTWEXBGS breaks below 118, EM assets and gold get additional tailwind but US corporate earnings face FX headwind
Curve steepening (2s10s +52bps) with 10Y at 4.33% — term premium rising, long-duration bonds are NOT safe despite recession fears

market_interpretation.geopolitical_risk: Elevated — Brent near $100 with 5-day avg >$104 implies active supply disruption or geopolitical premium; energy sector massively outperforming confirms market pricing sustained risk.

adjustments_output.cash_tactical_rationale: VIX at 22 and rising with regime uncertainty warrants tactical cash to buy dips if energy shock escalates or if equity capitulation creates opportunities; not excessive given T-bill yields ~4% in existing bond allocations.
